SEARCH
Package dev.jeka.core.api.tooling.docker

Class JkDocker

java.lang.Object
dev.jeka.core.api.system.JkAbstractProcess<JkDocker>
dev.jeka.core.api.tooling.docker.JkDocker
All Implemented Interfaces:
Runnable
public class JkDocker extends JkAbstractProcess<JkDocker>
Class providing utility methods for executing Docker commands.

  • Method Details

    • of

      public static JkDocker of()

    • copy

      protected JkDocker copy()
      Overrides:
      copy in class JkAbstractProcess<JkDocker>

    • login

      public JkDocker login(String registry, String username, String password)
      Login to a Docker registry using the specified credentials.
      Parameters:
      registry - The URL or name of the Docker registry to log in to, i.e. $ACR_NAME.azurecr.io
      username - The username for authentication with the registry.
      password - The password for authentication with the registry.

    • loginDockerHub

      public JkDocker loginDockerHub(String username, String password)

    • isPresent

      public boolean isPresent()
      Checks if Docker is present on the system.

    • assertPresent

      public JkDocker assertPresent()
      Asserts that Docker is present and responsive on the system.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the Docker daemon is not running or unresponsive.

    • getLocalImages

      public List<String> getLocalImages()
      Retrieves the names of Docker images available locally. The image names are formatted as "repository:tag".
      Returns:
      A set of strings representing the names of local Docker images, where each name is in the format "repository:tag".

    • prepareExecJekaInDocker

      public JkAbstractProcess<?> prepareExecJekaInDocker(Path workingDir, String... jekaCommandArgs)
      Prepares a JkProcess object to execute Jeka in a Docker container. This can be useful for creating native executable runnable in a container.

      The latest jekadev/jeka image will be used to execute jeka.
      The container will be map volumes to execute Jeka in the working directory, and to fetch cache in $HOME/.jeka/cache4c:/cache dir.
      You can force a specific version of Jeka by adding -Djeka.version=xxx to jekaCommandArgs

      Parameters:
      jekaCommandArgs - The argument to pass to JeKa

    • prepareExecJekaInDocker

      public JkAbstractProcess<?> prepareExecJekaInDocker(String... jekaCommandArgs)

    • execJekaInDocker

      public JkProcResult execJekaInDocker(String... jekaCommandArgs)
      Executes Jeka in a docker container.
      See Also:
      • prepareExecJekaInDocker(Path, String...)
